subject: DevOps
title: British Airways IT Meltdown (2017)
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on British Airways IT Meltdown (2017).

hook.text: May 2017. A power cut at BA's data center stranded 75,000 passengers for three days.

buildup.text: A contractor disconnected power. The uncontrolled restart surged and damaged servers.

discovery.text: Check-in, baggage, and flights all ran from one data center. One failure grounded everything.

twist.text: BA had a DR site but had never tested it at full load. It couldn't handle the demand.

climax.text: The outage cost BA over $100 million and triggered a massive regulatory fine.

punchline.text: Test your DR at full scale. Untested DR is a wish, not a plan.

quiz.question: Why did BA's disaster recovery site fail to help?
options: It had never been tested under full production load
It was in the same building as the primary site
The DR site's software was outdated
